questions.question: A can do a piece of work in 10 days and B in 15 days. How many days will they take to finish it together?
A can finish a job in 20 days. B is 25% more efficient than A. How many days does B take?
questions.explanation: B is 25% more efficient: B takes 20/1.25 = 16 days.

questions.question: A, B and C can complete a work in 20, 30 and 60 days respectively. In how many days will A complete the work if B and C assist him on alternate days?
questions.explanation: In 2 days: A works 2 days (2/20), B or C works 1 day alternately. Work per 2 days = 2/20+1/30+1/60 = 6/60+2/60+1/60=9/60=3/20. Days for full work = 2×20/3 = 13.33, so ≈ 15 days with rounding.

questions.question: A and B together can do a job in 12 days. A alone can do it in 20 days. How many days does B alone take?
A can do a work in 15 days and B in 20 days. They work together for 6 days and then A leaves. How many more days will B take to finish?
questions.explanation: Work done in 6 days = 6(1/15+1/20) = 6×7/60 = 42/60=7/10. Remaining=3/10. B takes 3/10×20=6 days. Answer: 6 days.

questions.question: 10 men can complete a work in 15 days. How many days will 25 men take to complete 5 times the work?
questions.explanation: Original work = 10×15=150 man-days. New work = 5×150=750 man-days. Time = 750/25=30 days.

questions.question: A can do a work in n days. B takes (n+5) days. Together they finish in 6 days. Find n.
Wages for 45 women for 48 days is Rs. 31,500. How many men must work for 16 days to earn Rs. 14,000, given a man earns double a woman per day?
questions.explanation: Woman wage/day = 31500/(45×48)≈14.58. Man wage/day ≈29.17. Men needed = 14000/(29.17×16)≈30. Closest=30.
